summary: This is a very brief blog post serving as a teaser for an upcoming series on writing specs, combined with a lighthearted anecdote about complaining to Starbucks customer service. Joel wrote to Starbucks about the grease pencils leaving black residue on his hands when he picks up his coffee. He received a prompt, professional reply from their customer relations team. The post is essentially filler content with a self-deprecating joke about drinking too much coffee.
key_points: Joel announces an upcoming series on how to write specs
He shares an anecdote about writing to Starbucks customer service about grease pencil residue on coffee cups
Starbucks responded promptly and professionally, forwarding his suggestion to operations
Joel jokes that excessive coffee consumption drives people to write complaint letters
money_quotes: See what drinking too much coffee makes you do?
key_insight: This is a minor transitional post teasing upcoming spec-writing content, padded with a humorous customer service anecdote.
